article_title: EU Council and Parliament Strike Provisional AI Act Omnibus Deal, Extending High-Risk Deadlines and Banning AI-Generated CSAM

findings.message: Body cites 1 URL(s) not in article.sources (provenance break)
findings.details:
These URLs appear as Markdown links in body_markdown but are missing from article.sources, so they have no committed snapshot under sources/YYYY-MM/<slug>/ and cannot be verified through the provenance chain. Orphan URL(s):
  - https://www.techpolicy.press/what-the-eu-ai-omnibus-deal-changes-for-the-eu-ai-act-and-what-lies-ahead/

content_preview.title: EU Council and Parliament Strike Provisional AI Act Omnibus Deal, Extending High-Risk Deadlines and Banning AI-Generated CSAM
content_preview.summary: Negotiators reached a provisional agreement at 4:30 a.m. on May 7, trading 16-month deadline extensions for a new prohibition on AI-generated non-consensual imagery.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Negotiators for the European Council and the European Parliament reached a provisional agreement in the early hours of May 7, 2026, on the first set of amendments to the EU AI Act since the regulation was adopted in June 2024. The deal — concluded at 4:30 a.m. after overnight negotiations, [according to TechPolicy.Press](https://www.techpolicy.press/what-the-eu-ai-omnibus-deal-changes-for-the-eu-ai-act-and-what-lies-ahead/) — forms part of Omnibus VII, one of ten simplification pack...

recommendations: Close the PR without merging. The work is discarded under the v3.9.0 workflow.

New submission required: fix the orphan URL by ensuring article.sources contains 'https://www.techpolicy.press/what-the-eu-ai-omnibus-deal-changes-for-the-eu-ai-act-and-what-lies-ahead/' (with 'eu-ai-act'), or update all body citations to use the URL that IS in article.sources ('for-the-ai-act' without 'eu-' prefix).

editor_notes.content_quality: Article content is substantively strong — detailed, well-structured coverage of the EU AI Act omnibus deal. However, it cannot be published due to a provenance break.
editor_notes.source_verification: Review terminated due to integrity failures. The submission has a critical orphan URL: the body_markdown links to 'https://www.techpolicy.press/what-the-eu-ai-omnibus-deal-changes-for-the-eu-ai-act-and-what-lies-ahead/' (with 'eu-ai-act' in path) in multiple places, but article.sources lists 'https://www.techpolicy.press/what-the-eu-ai-omnibus-deal-changes-for-the-ai-act-and-what-lies-ahead/' (without 'eu-' prefix). These are different URLs. The orphan URL has no committed source snapshot and breaks the provenance chain. Additionally, the reviewer's temporary submission copy had a hash mismatch because the URL was modified during file preparation — confirming the submissions are immutable signed artifacts.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Cannot assess — rejected due to provenance break before full source review.
concerns: Orphan URL in body_markdown not in article.sources: 'https://www.techpolicy.press/what-the-eu-ai-omnibus-deal-changes-for-the-eu-ai-act-and-what-lies-ahead/' appears in the body but article.sources has the slightly different 'https://www.techpolicy.press/what-the-eu-ai-omnibus-deal-changes-for-the-ai-act-and-what-lies-ahead/'. This is a provenance break that cannot be covered by a corrections note — requires a new submission.
recommendations: Resubmit with matching URLs between body_markdown and article.sources for the TechPolicy.Press article.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: REJECT — provenance break due to orphan URL. Article content is otherwise strong and worth resubmitting after fixing the URL mismatch.
